2606.30863.title: Beyond expert users: agents should help users construct preferences, not just elicit them
2606.30863.paper_summary: Agents typically assume an expert user -- one with well-formed preferences about what they want -- and default to clarifying questions whenever the task is underspecified. We argue this assumption is unrealistic. Users often lack the domain knowledge to have completely specified preferences; if asked about their preference on some feature, the user may be unable to answer without the agent helping the user to learn some domain knowledge needed to form a preference for that feature, e.g., via examples or explanations. To formalize these principles, we draw on the Search-Experience-Credence framework from Information Economics to introduce CoPref, a model of how users construct preferences based on agent dialog actions. We then study these ideas concretely in agentic recommender systems, proposing CoShop, an interactive benchmark. In CoShop, an agent converses with and makes recommendations for a CoPref user. The agent's performance depends on whether it can help the user gain the knowledge needed to specify the task well. Evaluating five frontier models, we find that no agent exceeds 56% accuracy on CoShop despite five turns of interaction. Failures stem not from agents' ability to find items, but from how little the interaction expands what users know about what they want.
2606.30863.paper_summary_zh: 代理通常假設用戶是專家用戶——擁有明確的偏好——並在任務不明確時默認使用澄清問題。我們認為這一假設不切實際。用戶往往缺乏領域知識，無法擁有完全明確的偏好；如果詢問他們對某個特徵的偏好，用戶可能無法回答，除非代理幫助用戶學習形成該特徵偏好所需的一些領域知識，例如通過示例或解釋。為了形式化這些原則，我們借鑒了信息經濟學中的搜索-體驗-信任框架，介紹了CoPref，一個用戶如何根據代理對話行為構建偏好的模型。我們然後在代理推薦系統中具體研究這些想法，提出了CoShop，一個互動基準。在CoShop中，代理與CoPref用戶進行對話並提供推薦。代理的表現取決於它是否能幫助用戶獲得指定任務所需的知識。評估五個前沿模型時，我們發現儘管有五輪互動，沒有任何代理在CoShop上的準確率超過56%。失敗的原因並不在於代理尋找項目的能力，而在於互動對用戶了解他們想要的東西的擴展程度非常有限。
2606.30863.author: Irena Saracay et.al.
2606.30863.authors: Irena Saracay, Ludwig Schmidt, Carlos Guestrin
